title: Brand Configuration
description: Brand-scope configuration (brands/<brand-id>/brand.json). The brand-keyed structured-config layer (DD-10 / RD-5). One file per brand. Replaces production live-config files by generalizing their shapes; only the template ships, with NO real brand values. Spec ref: release-spec.md §11.3.

schema_version.description: Version of this config shape, for migration handling (§11.1).

id.description: Stable brand id. Matches the brands/<brand-id>/ directory name and the 'brand' key used across artifacts.

account_class.description: operator (a founder/operator-owned account, looser media strictness) vs brand (a brand/project account).

platforms.description: Per-platform publishing configuration for this brand.

cooldown_overrides.description: Per-brand override of the system cooldown defaults (§8.6). Absent means inherit system.json cooldown.

drama_dial.description: Per-brand dramatization stance consumed by the voice/style rules. The brand-DNA authoring template carries the matching preference question. VOICE-CALIBRATION AXIS 1: this is a STRUCTURED voice field; it is managed by the voice-calibration pathway (engine voice-calibrate) and is always target_mutability:human-only — the self-improve machine applier MUST refuse it (EHUMANONLY). Changes to this field ONLY via the consent-gated apply path (engine voice-calibrate --apply --consent).

archetype_emphasis.description: VOICE-CALIBRATION AXIS 2: a ranked list of content archetype codes the brand leans into, with associated weights. Managed by the voice-calibration pathway (engine voice-calibrate); always target_mutability:human-only. Absent means no explicit weighting — the engine uses the flat distribution. Pattern LABELS only (archetype code strings); never verbatim copy. Changes only via consent-gated apply (engine voice-calibrate --apply --consent).

code.description: Archetype code from the engine archetype taxonomy (e.g. HOW_TO, SKY_TONIGHT, THESIS_OR_RECEIPT). Labels only.

weight.description: Relative weight for this archetype (higher = more emphasis). Weights need not sum to 1; the engine normalizes.

hook_preferences.description: VOICE-CALIBRATION AXIS 3: preferred opening-hook patterns with associated weights. Managed by the voice-calibration pathway; always target_mutability:human-only. Pattern LABELS only (e.g. 'direct-tip', 'how-to-numbered', 'question-hook') — NEVER verbatim competitor text. Changes only via consent-gated apply (engine voice-calibrate --apply --consent).

pattern.description: A label for the preferred opening-hook pattern (e.g. 'direct-tip', 'how-to-numbered', 'question-hook', 'announcement-breaking'). Label strings only — never competitor copy.

weight.description: Relative weight for this hook pattern (higher = more preferred). The engine normalizes.

cadence_preferences.description: VOICE-CALIBRATION AXIS 4: posting cadence and structural preferences. Managed by the voice-calibration pathway; always target_mutability:human-only. Structured counts/ratios only — never verbatim copy. Changes only via consent-gated apply (engine voice-calibrate --apply --consent).

preferred_posts_per_week.description: Preferred posting frequency (posts per week) for this brand.

thread_preference.description: Preference for thread / multi-part format relative to standalone posts.

media_preference.description: Preference for including media (images, video, GIFs) in posts.

top_days.description: Preferred day-of-week labels (mon–sun) sorted by posting priority descending.

voice_calibration.description: OPTIONAL state object tracking the most recent voice-calibration cycle for this brand (roadmap #5). Written by the engine voice-calibrate pathway; never hand-edited. Records the scan that sourced the proposal, the four-axis current and proposed values at proposal time, consent status, and apply metadata. Written alongside the learning record at proposal/apply time. ALWAYS target_mutability:human-only — the self-improve machine applier MUST refuse it.

scan_id.description: The learning record id of the voice-calibration proposal derived from the most recent competitor scan.

current.description: Snapshot of the four voice axes as they stood when the proposal was generated (for diffing and rollback).

proposed.description: Proposed four-axis values from the most recent calibration proposal.

consented.description: Whether the operator has explicitly consented to apply the proposal (via engine voice-calibrate --apply --consent). False until consent is given; the apply step refuses without consent (ECONSENTREQUIRED).

applied_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the proposal was applied (voice fields written to brand.json). Null until applied.

rollback_ref.description: Instance-repo git commit ref captured before the apply (the pre-apply HEAD), enabling one-step rollback via engine voice-calibrate --rollback. Null until applied.

work_recap.description: OPTIONAL per-brand work-recap / build-in-public settings (release-spec §3.3). Meaningful only for account_class=operator brands (founder/operator/team accounts share the build in the open with flexible voice). CONFIG-GATED and OFF BY DEFAULT — enable the pathway in system.json work_recap; this block scopes a brand's own memory source and private-term deny list when system.json work_recap.accounts references it (or single-account mode points at this brand). PRIVACY IS LOAD-BEARING: the repo ships the MECHANISM pointed at a configured path; it NEVER bundles real memory.

enabled.description: Per-brand opt-in (still subject to the system.json work_recap.enabled master gate; both must be true). Defaults false.

memory_path.description: Path to this account's SENSITIVE memory the source scans (read-only; never copied into the repo).

files.description: Files-glob relative to memory_path. Supports an exact file and a single 'dir/*.ext' wildcard. Default ['MEMORY.md', 'memory/*.md'].

lookback_days.description: How many days back the scan considers recent work. Default 3.

private_terms.description: Per-brand private-term deny list (partner names, codenames, unreleased feature names) — PUBLIC anti-targets to NOT print, unioned with the system-level list. Never secret VALUES. Either a flat string array or a { terms, secret_literals } object.

ingestion.description: OPTIONAL per-brand DATA-INGESTION & BRAND-IDENTITY settings (release-spec §1.1/§1.2; DD-10 brand-keyed). Consumed by the one-command brand-DNA flow in the C2 setup checkpoint when system.json brand_dna.enabled is true (both gates must hold). OFF BY DEFAULT — absent means the brand uses the manual authoring path only (DD-21). Names WHICH accounts to ingest the own-corpus from and WHICH competitors to analyze for patterns; competitor content is Zone U (RD-8) and analyzed for PATTERNS only, never republished verbatim. Handles are public anti-targets/identifiers, never secrets. Scraping is BYO (RD-9): a per-brand scraper.adapter ref may override the system default; manual submission + EXPORTS to manual_corpus_path are first-class and need no adapter.

account_handles.description: Per-platform OWN-account handle(s) the own-corpus is ingested from (release-spec §1.1 Account Linking). Synthetic placeholders in shipped templates (e.g. @acmecosmos). Public identifiers, never secrets.

competitors.description: Competitor/comparator handles to ingest as Zone-U corpus and analyze for PATTERNS (release-spec §1.2 Competitor Identification). Each is untrusted third-party (RD-8) — analyzed for archetypes/hooks only, NEVER republished verbatim. Public handles, never secrets.

manual_corpus_path.description: CONTENT_HOME-relative directory the operator drops MANUAL / EXPORTED corpus items into (the first-class non-scraped path, RD-9). Items conform to schemas/inputs/corpus-item.schema.json. Defaults to the brand's corpora dir when absent.

scraper.description: OPTIONAL per-brand override of the system brand_dna.scraper adapter ref (DD-10). Absent inherits the system default; still BYO (RD-9, no creds bundled) and still a metered action gated by the cost estimate (DD-18).

adapter.description: Registered scraper-adapter name for this brand (overrides system default). The manual/export path needs no adapter.

provider.description: Adapter-specific provider config (§12.5-style, opaque to the engine). Credential resolves BY NAME via secrets.js; no credential VALUE here.

retention_class.description: Default retention_class stamped on this brand's ingested corpus items at write (corpus-item.schema.json). Governs engine purge-corpora windows (system.json retention). Defaults to 'standard'; competitor corpus is typically 'transient' so it ages out fast.

paths.description: CONTENT_HOME-relative locations of this brand's authored assets. Absolute paths are forbidden.

corpora.description: Ingested corpora directory for this brand.

publisher.description: Which publisher adapter carries this platform for this brand.

integration_ref.description: The integration id the publisher shows in its settings for the connected account, recorded at account-connection time. For Postiz-backed lanes this is the Postiz integration id. Giphy lanes leave it null and use the Giphy credentials/username instead. Null until the account is connected (deferrable until LIVE).

handle_placeholder.description: Display handle for this account. Synthetic placeholder in shipped templates (e.g. @acmecosmos).

rate_limit_per_day.description: Maximum posts per day for this brand×platform. Enforced by the publish gate.

publish_windows.description: Allowed local-time publish windows. Outside these windows the publish gate fails unless the queue entry is marked publish-immediately. Empty/absent means no window restriction.

auto_publish.description: RD-10 auto-publish trust-state block — engine-maintained per brand×platform.

end.description: Window end, local HH:MM 24h. May be earlier than start to express a window that crosses midnight.

auto_publish_state.description: Auto-publish removes the publisher second gate for this lane (never the human approval). Entry/revocation are enforced mechanically by the engine (§15.5); operators set 'enabled', the engine maintains the rest.

enabled.description: Operator opt-in. The engine still requires the trust criteria to be met before publishing automatically.

qualifying_streak.description: Consecutive qualifying publishes on this lane (engine-maintained). Reset to 0 on revocation.

last_revocation.description: Timestamp of the most recent automatic revocation, or null.

revocation_reason.description: Reason for the most recent revocation, or null.

relative_path.description: CONTENT_HOME-relative path. Absolute paths and drive letters are forbidden.

platform.description: Platform the handle belongs to (twitter, instagram, youtube, tiktok, facebook, ...).

handle.description: The public account handle (synthetic placeholder in shipped templates, e.g. @acmecosmos). Never a secret.

name.description: Human-readable competitor/comparator name (Zone U, RD-8). Synthetic in shipped templates. Public identifier; analyzed for patterns only, never republished.

handles.description: The competitor's public per-platform handle(s) to ingest as Zone-U corpus.
